Phase-70.NAME: VPN QR Parity and Config Page Compaction
Phase-70.purpose: Make the user-cabinet VPN QR code visually lighter and provably equivalent to the working VPN config path, while keeping the config page compact after removing diagnostics/raw-config panels.

scope.in: QR payload audit, frontend QR rendering parameters, backend QR endpoint comparison, CLI create-client QR comparison, decode/roundtrip tests, config page compaction verification, and mobile scanning UX.
scope.out: Changing VPN keys, AWG obfuscation profile, deploy/create-client.sh behavior, install/deploy scripts, server topology, .conf download MIME hardening, or removing required config lines without deterministic proof.

requirements.requirement-1: Before changing QR rendering, prove whether the user-cabinet QR payload equals the authenticated config text and how it differs from CLI create-client output.
requirements.requirement-2: If payloads differ unexpectedly, fix the API/config selection path instead of only changing QR cosmetics.
requirements.requirement-3: If payloads are correct but QR is visually too dense, tune QR rendering parameters such as error correction level, margin, size, and scaling while preserving scan reliability.
requirements.requirement-4: Do not delete WireGuard/AmneziaWG config fields merely to reduce QR density unless tests prove the resulting config is equivalent and compatible.
requirements.requirement-5: The frontend QR and backend /vpn/config/qr route must decode back to the exact intended config payload.
requirements.requirement-6: The config page must stay compact and must not reintroduce config-diagnostics or data-phase57-raw-config after Phase-68 removes them.
requirements.requirement-7: QR, .conf download, copy, and device management actions must remain reachable and finger-safe on mobile.

steps.step-1: Audit backend VPN config generation, /vpn/config, /vpn/config/qr, /vpn/config/download, device-bound config bundles, and deploy/create-client.sh QR generation.
steps.step-2: Add deterministic test helpers to compare API config text, device config text, backend QR payload, and CLI-compatible config payload without logging private keys.
steps.step-3: If config payload mismatch exists, align the user-cabinet config source with the canonical VPN config contract and preserve Phase-48 download headers.
steps.step-4: If payload is correct, tune frontend QRCodeCanvas settings toward a lighter visual QR while preserving decode reliability and reduced mobile crowding.
steps.step-5: Tune backend QR endpoint settings only if it remains part of the active contract; otherwise document frontend-owned QR rendering and keep backend route compatibility.
steps.step-6: Keep the compact config page free of diagnostics/raw-config panels while preserving QR modal, .conf download, copy, device create/rotate/revoke, and unavailable states.
steps.step-7: Add QR decode/roundtrip tests or smoke checks that confirm generated PNG/canvas payloads decode to the expected config and never expose payload material in logs.
steps.step-8: Run frontend build, VPN router compatibility tests, Phase-48/57/62 smokes, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/runtime guard.

data-flow.flow-1: User opens /dashboard/config -> frontend fetches config/device bundle -> QR modal renders exact config payload -> user scans with AmneziaWG.
data-flow.flow-2: User downloads .conf -> M-066 returns application/octet-stream attachment -> downloaded file remains the same payload contract as QR/copy where applicable.
data-flow.flow-3: Operator runs create-client.sh -> backend CLI writes config -> qrencode renders terminal/PNG QR -> Phase-70 compares behavior without modifying deploy script.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: Payloads match and QR is dense only because error correction is high: Phase-70 lowers/tunes rendering within scan-safe limits and proves decode parity.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Payloads differ because the user cabinet includes extra or wrong config material: Phase-70 fixes source parity before any visual tuning.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: Mobile user opens config page after compaction: QR, .conf, copy, and device actions remain visible without diagnostics/raw-config clutter.

verification-design.gate-1: Backend tests must verify QR/config/download payload parity and route compatibility without printing private keys or configs.
verification-design.gate-2: Frontend smoke must verify QR modal is present, lighter rendering settings are applied, and removed config panels stay absent.
verification-design.gate-3: Phase-48 smoke must continue to prove .conf download MIME/filename safeguards after any config-page edits.
verification-design.gate-4: Phase-57/62 smokes must confirm config workflow remains reachable after compaction.
verification-design.gate-5: Protected diff guard must fail if deploy/create-client.sh, install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, VPN runtime topology, or AWG profile are changed without explicit approval.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py tests/test_vpn_service.py tests/test_manual_client_cli.py -v
python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-70.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml
mygrace lint; git diff --check; protected deploy/install/runtime diff guard

risk-assessment.risk-1: Reducing QR density by changing config content can silently break existing VPN connectivity; payload parity must be proven first.
risk-assessment.risk-2: Lower error correction can make QR easier-looking but less scannable on low-quality screens; decode and device-oriented checks are required.
risk-assessment.risk-3: QR smoke output must not leak private keys, config payloads, server credentials, or user identifiers.
risk-assessment.risk-4: Removing raw config fallback improves compactness but can reduce troubleshooting paths; QR/download/copy must remain reliable.

Phase-70.acceptance: User-cabinet QR is visually lighter only after payload parity is proven, config page remains compact without diagnostics/raw config, and QR/download/copy/device workflows pass backend/frontend/MyGRACE verification.
